requirements.requirement_text: Credentials must never be transmitted or stored in plaintext.

supporting_evidence.title: Password Storage Standard
supporting_evidence.evidence_snippet: Application passwords must be stored only as salted cryptographic hashes and must never be stored or transmitted in plaintext.
supporting_evidence.supports_requirement: This policy explicitly prohibits storing or transmitting passwords in plaintext.

requirements.reasoning: This requirement is satisfied because CP-002 explicitly prohibits storing or transmitting passwords in plaintext.

requirements.requirement_text: Authentication attempts must be rate-limited to reduce brute-force exposure.

supporting_evidence.title: Login Rate Protection
supporting_evidence.evidence_snippet: Internet-facing login services must implement rate limiting or account lockout mechanisms to reduce brute-force attacks.
supporting_evidence.supports_requirement: This policy requires rate limiting or account lockout mechanisms to prevent brute-force attacks.

requirements.reasoning: This requirement is partially satisfied because CP-006 requires rate limiting or account lockout mechanisms, but it does not explicitly mention authentication attempts.
requirements.recommended_action: Implement rate limiting or account lockout mechanisms for all authentication attempts, not just internet-facing login services.

requirements.requirement_text: Default vendor credentials must be changed prior to production deployment.

supporting_evidence.title: Vendor Account Hardening
supporting_evidence.evidence_snippet: All default usernames and vendor-supplied passwords must be changed before any server or application is moved into production.
supporting_evidence.supports_requirement: This policy requires changing default vendor credentials before production deployment.

requirements.reasoning: This requirement is satisfied because CP-003 explicitly requires changing default vendor credentials before production deployment.

requirements.requirement_text: Authentication events must be logged with timestamp, source IP, and outcome.

supporting_evidence.title: Centralized Authentication Logging
supporting_evidence.evidence_snippet: Every successful and failed login attempt must be recorded with timestamp, source IP address, username, and authentication outcome.
supporting_evidence.supports_requirement: This policy requires logging authentication events with timestamp, source IP address, username, and authentication outcome.

requirements.reasoning: This requirement is partially satisfied because CP-001 requires logging authentication events with timestamp, source IP address, username, and authentication outcome, but it does not explicitly mention source IP.
requirements.recommended_action: Modify CP-001 to explicitly require logging source IP.

requirements.requirement_text: Session tokens issued after authentication must expire after a defined inactivity period.
requirements.reasoning: There is no evidence in the retrieved policies that session tokens expire after a defined inactivity period.
requirements.recommended_action: Implement session token expiration after a defined inactivity period.

requirements.requirement_text: Authentication mechanisms must be reviewed at least annually for continued adequacy.

supporting_evidence.title: Authentication Security Review
supporting_evidence.evidence_snippet: Authentication systems shall undergo a documented security review every year to verify their continued effectiveness.
supporting_evidence.supports_requirement: This policy requires reviewing authentication mechanisms at least annually.

requirements.reasoning: This requirement is satisfied because CP-005 explicitly requires reviewing authentication mechanisms at least annually.

requirements.requirement_text: Fallback or legacy authentication paths must be disabled unless formally approved.

supporting_evidence.title: Legacy Authentication Exception
supporting_evidence.evidence_snippet: Legacy authentication protocols may remain enabled if they are required by older business applications.
supporting_evidence.supports_requirement: This policy allows legacy authentication protocols to remain enabled under certain conditions.

requirements.reasoning: This requirement is partially satisfied because CP-004 allows legacy authentication protocols to remain enabled under certain conditions, but it does not explicitly require disabling fallback or legacy authentication paths unless formally approved.
requirements.recommended_action: Modify CP-004 to explicitly require disabling fallback or legacy authentication paths unless formally approved.

requirements.reasoning: The Account Provisioning Policy requires MFA setup as part of the onboarding process, but does not explicitly state that MFA enrollment must occur before production access is granted. This requirement is partially satisfied.
requirements.recommended_action: Update the Account Provisioning Policy to explicitly require MFA enrollment before production access is granted.

requirements.requirement_text: MFA bypass or exemption requests must be approved by the security team and time-limited.
requirements.reasoning: There is no evidence in the retrieved policies to support this requirement.
requirements.recommended_action: Develop a policy that outlines the process for MFA bypass or exemption requests, including approval by the security team and time-limited exemptions.

requirements.requirement_text: Backup MFA methods must be reviewed to ensure they do not weaken overall assurance.
requirements.reasoning: There is no evidence in the retrieved policies to support this requirement.
requirements.recommended_action: Develop a policy that outlines the review process for backup MFA methods to ensure they do not weaken overall assurance.

requirements.requirement_text: MFA prompts must include contextual information to reduce approval fatigue and push-bombing risk.
requirements.reasoning: There is no evidence in the retrieved policies to support this requirement.
requirements.recommended_action: Develop a policy that outlines the requirements for MFA prompts to include contextual information.

next_actions: Update the Account Provisioning Policy to explicitly require MFA enrollment before production access is granted.
Develop a policy that outlines the process for MFA bypass or exemption requests, including approval by the security team and time-limited exemptions.
Develop a policy that outlines the review process for backup MFA methods to ensure they do not weaken overall assurance.
Develop a policy that outlines the requirements for MFA prompts to include contextual information.
